paragraphs.text: Common Statistical Analysis Plan Template v5

runs.text: ommon Statistical Analysis Plan Template v

paragraphs.text: About This Template
Disclaimer

This document is a template for a statistical analysis plan (SAP). It contains sections marked as common text that may be used across SAPs with little to no editing if the user chooses to do so. The use of this template is at the discretion of the user. Recommendations for modifications in future releases of the common SAP template can be submitted at any time and will be reviewed on a routine basis.

paragraphs.text: Components of the SAP Template

The Core Backbone and Appendices are designed to cover, at minimum, the needs for regulatory reporting and the clinical study report (CSR). Levels 1 and 2 headings are aligned with Section 9 Statistical Considerations in the common protocol template (CPT). Level 3 and lower headings can be deleted/added/modified if additional planned analyses need to be described.

paragraphs.text: The Core Backbone contains analysis plan information common to all phases, study populations, and therapeutic areas. The core backbone is streamlined and focused on the regulatory reporting recommendations and/or requirements.

paragraphs.text: Appendices provide additional information that can be accessed when needed, eg, abbreviations, data handling conventions, CSR items that are not critical for prespecification

paragraphs.text: Core Backbone Headings

Levels 1 and 2 headings should be consistent across analysis plans that use the TransCelerate common SAP template for reference purposes. The structure of this template was chosen to maximize alignment with the statistical section of the Common Protocol Template.

runs.text: 1 and 2 headings should be consistent across analysis plans that use the TransCelerate
ommon SAP template for reference purposes.
statistical section of the Common Protocol Template.

paragraphs.text: Levels 1 and 2 headings should not be deleted. If they are not relevant to the study, not applicable should be inserted so that the numbering of subsequent sections is not changed.

paragraphs.text: Level 3 and subsequent subheadings are suggested and can be deleted/added/modified as needed.
Terminology
The terminology used in the SAP should match the protocol.

runs.text: The terminology used in the SAP should match the protocol.

paragraphs.text: The following terminology has been selected for use within TransCelerate common templates (CPT, SAP, and CSR) and is considered to be appropriate for all phases, study populations, and therapeutic areas.

runs.text: The following terminology has been selected for use within
TransCelerate common templates (CPT, SAP, and CSR)
and is considered to be appropriate for all phases, study populations, and therapeutic areas.

paragraphs.text: Participant is used rather than subject, healthy volunteer, or patient.

runs.text: is used rather than subject, healthy volunteer, or patient.

paragraphs.text: Study intervention is used rather than study drug. Study interventions are all prespecified, investigational and noninvestigational medicinal products*, medical devices and other interventions (eg, surgical and behavioral) intended to be administered to the study participants during the study conduct. Procedures conducted to manage participants or to collect data are excluded from the usage of this term. Investigational interventions are a subset of study interventions that are being tested or used as a control (eg, placebo or active comparator).

runs.text: are all prespecified, investigational and noninvestigational medicinal products*, medical devices and other interventions (eg, surgical and behavioral) intended to be administered to the study participants during the study conduct.
Procedures conducted to manage participants or to collect data are excluded from the usage of this term.
are a subset of study interventions that are being tested or used as a control (eg, placebo or active comparator).

paragraphs.text: *Please be referred to Eudralex - Volume 10 - Clinical trials guidelines (Chapter III) for further information on some categories of medicinal products that are normally used in clinical trials as NIMPs (noninvestigational medicinal products), such as rescue medication, challenge agents, medicinal products used to assess endpoints in the clinical trial, concomitant medicinal products systematically prescribed to the study patients, and background treatment.

runs.text: *Please be referred to Eudralex - Volume 10 - Clinical trials guidelines (Chapter III) for further information on some categories of medicinal products
are normally used in clinical trials as NIMPs

, such as rescue medication, challenge agents, medicinal products used to assess endpoints in the clinical trial, concomitant medicinal products systematically prescribed to the study patients, and background treatment.

paragraphs.text: Of note, all products administered during the study that are not study interventions are recorded as concomitant therapy.

runs.text: Of note, all products administered during the study that are not study interventions are recorded as concomitant therapy.

paragraphs.text: Formatting and Text Conventions

Common Text: Black font preceded by <Start of common text> and followed by <End of common text> is common language intended to be harmonized across SAPs. The recommendation is to use this text as written to maintain consistency across template users, but the text can be adapted or deleted, if required. Driver: Industry regulation or guidance.

runs.text: Common Text: Black font preceded by <Start of common text> and followed by <End of common text> is common
language intended to be harmonized across SAPs. The recommendation is to use this text as written to maintain consistency across template users, but the text can be adapted or deleted, if required.

paragraphs.text: Suggested Text: Black text preceded by <Start of suggested text> and followed by <End of suggested text> is suggested language that can be adapted or deleted as needed. Authors should consider implications to reuse within the CTS or the CT Registry if text is to be modified or removed.

runs.text: Suggested Text: Black text preceded by <Start of suggested text> and followed by <End of suggested text> is suggested language that can be adapted or deleted as needed. Authors should consider implications to reuse within the CTS or the CT Registry if text is to be modified or removed.

paragraphs.text: Variable Text: Blue bracketed text is variable text that should be addressed based on individual study needs.

runs.text: Variable Text: Blue bracketed text is variable text that should be addressed based on individual study needs.

paragraphs.text: Example Text: Green italicized text preceded by <Start of example text> and followed by <End of example text> should be removed by the author if not utilized.

> should be removed by the author if not utilized.

paragraphs.text: Instructional Text: Intended to aid in the authoring of the SAP. In the basic Word edition, it is red, hidden text, and paragraph marks must be enabled in order for it to be displayed. In the technology-enabled edition, it will appear only in the instructional text panel.

paragraphs.text: The flags for the start and end of common, suggested, or example text can be removed automatically at the time of SAP finalization if the eSAP template has been used or should be removed manually by the author if the Basic Word Edition SAP template has been used.

paragraphs.text: Disclosure

Users are reminded that many regulators have begun to require increased public disclosure of clinical trial documents. For recommendations related to using this template given the disclosure requirements, please refer to the best practices for disclosure in the implementation toolkit available on TransCelerate’s website.

For recommendations related to using this template given the disclosure requirements, please refer to the best practices for disclosure in the implementation toolkit available on TransCelerate’s website.

Version History

This section should include high-level, concise documentation of any changes from previous versions of the SAP. Include a sentence at the beginning of this section that references the current version of the protocol with the version date.

paragraphs.text: For amendments to the SAP, describe the reason the SAP is amended such as: protocol amendment, changes following blinded data review, request from the data monitoring committee, etc. Details on the major changes and rationale for these changes for each SAP amendment are included in this section. A tabular format like the example table provided below may be used to present this information. Additional rows may be added to the table for the SAP amendment to describe the changes.

Introduction
Objectives
Objectives, estimands, and endpoint information should be copied directly from Section 3 of the CPT. More estimands may be added to this section.

paragraphs.text: Planned interim analyses.

Describe when the primary analysis will be performed, eg, at database lock after last participant last visit, at 100% primary endpoint data availability. Also describe final analysis timing if there will be subsequent reporting events, eg, after long-term follow-up.
